summary: Tested SwarmSim tiered LLM strategy. CRITICAL ISSUE: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite model (gemini-2.5-flash-lite) is NOT available in the emergent integration. The simulation fails because call_flash() cannot generate posts/replies/narratives. Premium (Sonnet 4) and Fast (Haiku 4.5) models work correctly for intel briefs, graph extraction, agent generation, and critic checks. Frontend loads correctly with Document Upload / Live Intelligence toggle.

critical.issue: Gemini 2.5 Flash Lite model (gemini-2.5-flash-lite) is NOT available in emergent integration. Error: 'Invalid model name passed in model=gemini/gemini-2.5-flash-lite. Call /v1/models to view available models for your key.' This breaks ALL simulation post/reply/narrative generation.

passed_tests: GET /api/sessions/{id}/live-status returns completed with graph (17 entities)
Intel brief generated successfully (uses call_premium - Sonnet 4)
Critic brief check works (uses call_fast - Haiku 4.5)
Frontend loads with Document Upload / Live Intelligence toggle
Frontend Live Intelligence view shows topic input, horizon options, Fetch & Analyze button
failed_tests: POST /api/sessions/{id}/simulate - Simulation completes but generates 0 posts because call_flash fails
Batched post generation fails - gemini-2.5-flash-lite model not available
Batched reply generation fails - gemini-2.5-flash-lite model not available
Round narrative generation fails - gemini-2.5-flash-lite model not available
action_items: CRITICAL: Fix call_flash() model - gemini-2.5-flash-lite is NOT available in emergent integration
Option 1: Change call_flash to use a different Gemini model that IS available (e.g., gemini-1.5-flash, gemini-2.0-flash)
Option 2: Fall back to Haiku 4.5 for flash tier if Gemini is not available
Option 3: Check /v1/models endpoint to see what Gemini models ARE available and use one of those
After fixing, re-test simulation to verify posts are generated with is_hub_post, belief_position, emotional_valence fields
critical_code_review_comments: The three-tier LLM architecture is well-designed: Premium (Sonnet 4) for deep reasoning, Fast (Haiku 4.5) for medium tasks, Flash (Gemini) for bulk generation
Orchestrator correctly receives call_fns dict with premium/fast/flash keys
call_premium and call_fast work correctly with emergent integration
call_flash fails because gemini-2.5-flash-lite model is not available in the emergent integration
The _llm_call function has good retry logic (3 retries with exponential backoff)
Batched post/reply generation logic in server.py lines 1103-1230 is correct but cannot execute due to model unavailability

success_rate.backend: 60% (Premium and Fast tiers work, Flash tier fails)

test_credentials: No auth required. Uses EMERGENT_LLM_KEY from backend .env.
seed_data_creation: None - tests create their own sessions
context_for_next_testing_agent: The tiered LLM strategy has a CRITICAL bug: call_flash() uses gemini-2.5-flash-lite which is NOT available in the emergent integration. Premium (Sonnet 4) and Fast (Haiku 4.5) work correctly. The simulation runs but generates 0 posts because all post/reply/narrative generation uses call_flash. Main agent needs to either: (1) use a different Gemini model that IS available, (2) fall back to Haiku for flash tier, or (3) check available models via /v1/models endpoint.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: The gemini-2.5-flash-lite model is not available in the emergent LLM integration. The error message states: 'Invalid model name passed in model=gemini/gemini-2.5-flash-lite. Call /v1/models to view available models for your key.'
reproduction_steps: 2. Fetch live data via POST /api/sessions/{id}/fetch-live (this works - uses Premium)
3. Generate agents via POST /api/sessions/{id}/generate-agents (this works - uses Premium)
5. Observe that simulation completes with 0 posts because call_flash fails
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Change the model in call_flash() from 'gemini-2.5-flash-lite' to an available model. Options: (1) Use gemini-1.5-flash or gemini-2.0-flash if available, (2) Fall back to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (same as call_fast), (3) Query /v1/models to find available Gemini models
